  • API Reference
  • Python Interop
  • Python Interop


Python Interop

KWave.jl can construct Python-compatible k-Wave objects for use with the Python k-Wave package via juliacall. This allows running the Python k-Wave simulation engine while building the simulation setup in Julia.

Functions

KWave.python_kwave_grid — Function
python_kwave_grid(nx, dx, ny=nothing, dy=nothing, nz=nothing, dz=nothing)

Python-friendly grid constructor. Dispatches to the appropriate KWaveGrid based on which dimensions are provided.

Usage from Python:

from juliacall import Main as jl
jl.seval("using KWave")
grid = jl.KWave.python_kwave_grid(128, 1e-4, 128, 1e-4)
source
KWave.python_medium — Function
python_medium(; sound_speed, density=1.0, kwargs...)

Python-friendly medium constructor. Accepts plain numbers and numpy arrays (which juliacall auto-converts to Julia arrays).

source
KWave.python_source — Function
python_source(; kwargs...)

Python-friendly source constructor.

source
KWave.python_sensor — Function
python_sensor(; mask=nothing, record=[:p], kwargs...)

Python-friendly sensor constructor.

source
KWave.python_run_simulation — Function
python_run_simulation(grid, medium, source, sensor; kwargs...)

Python-friendly simulation runner. Returns results as a Dict that juliacall maps to a Python dict.

source
KWave.python_output_to_dict — Function
python_output_to_dict(output::SimulationOutput)

Convert SimulationOutput to a Dict{String, Array} for Python consumption.

source

Workflow

using KWave

# Build Julia-side objects
kgrid  = KWaveGrid(128, 0.1e-3, 128, 0.1e-3)
make_time!(kgrid, 1500.0)
medium = KWaveMedium(sound_speed=1500.0)
source = KWaveSource(p0=rand(128, 128))
sensor = KWaveSensor()

# Convert to Python k-Wave objects
py_grid   = python_kwave_grid(kgrid)
py_medium = python_medium(medium)
py_source = python_source(source)
py_sensor = python_sensor(sensor)

# Run via Python k-Wave engine
output = python_run_simulation(py_grid, py_medium, py_source, py_sensor)

# Convert output back to Julia Dict
result = python_output_to_dict(output)
« Reference Solutions
